name: Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm
name_original: Ascenso al puerto de Tudons y Confrides — Circular desde Benidorm
region_intro: Road cycling around Benidorm features a diverse landscape, combining extensive coastal routes with challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by prominent natural parks like Sierra Helada and Sierra de Aitana, offering varied terrain from sea-level paths to high mountain passes. Cyclists encounter winding roads, steep climbs, and panoramic views across the Mediterranean Sea and inland valleys. The area's well-maintained paved roads cater to a range of abilities, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs. Best road cycling routes around Benidorm The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm, a 48.4 miles (77.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guadalest Castle – Guadalest Reservoir loop from l'Alfàs del Pi, a difficult 41.0 miles (65.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Guadalest reservoir and surrounding mountains, including challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Polop de la Marina – View of Puig Campana loop from l'Alfàs del Pi, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with views of Puig Campana, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Road cycling around Benidorm is defined by coastal paths, mountain ascents in Sierra Helada and Sierra de Aitana, and scenic valley routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 20,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Benidorm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Benidorm?
A: Benidorm offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from challenging mountain ascents to more relaxed coastal rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Benidorm?
A: Thanks to its favorable climate, Benidorm is an excellent destination for road cycling almost every day of the year. The mild winters and sunny conditions make it particularly appealing outside of peak summer heat.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Benidorm?
A: You'll find a diverse landscape for road cycling in Benidorm. The region features stunning coastal routes offering continuous sea views, challenging mountain ascents in areas like Sierra Helada and Sierra de Aitana, and picturesque routes through inland valleys with views of reservoirs and historic towns.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Benidorm provides routes for all abilities. There are 11 easy routes for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride, 16 moderate routes for intermediate cyclists, and 25 difficult routes for experienced riders looking for significant climbs and longer distances.

Q: Which routes offer significant mountain climbs and elevation gain?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes heading towards Sierra de Aitana, the highest mountain in Alicante, offer demanding climbs. Notable routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890794716/ascent-to-the-port-of-tudons-and-confrides-circular-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1,700 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33066029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates – View of Vall de Pop loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt;, with nearly 1,900 meters of climbing.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes with scenic coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Benidorm area offer beautiful coastal vistas. You can enjoy relaxed, flat rides along the Paseo de Poniente in Benidorm itself, or explore longer routes that extend through charming coastal towns like Altea, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33024355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Altea and Marina – Moraira Beach loop from Benidorm Intermodal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Benidorm?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Benidorm are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890794716/ascent-to-the-port-of-tudons-and-confrides-circular-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24971276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Castle – Guadalest Reservoir loop from l'Alfàs del Pi&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by significant landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Guadalest and the Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/497288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The region's natural parks, such as Sierra Helada, also offer dramatic cliffside views of the Mediterranean.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Benidorm?
A: The road cycling routes in Benidorm are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to beautiful coastal roads, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that ensure a smooth riding experience.

Q: Are there bike-friendly services available in Benidorm?
A: Benidorm is recognized for its bike-friendly environment, offering specialized services for cyclists. You can find bike rentals, repair workshops, and hotels tailored to cyclists' needs, ensuring a comfortable and convenient experience.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Benidorm offers 11 easy and 16 moderate road cycling routes. While specific family-friendly routes with very short distances are not highlighted, the easier coastal paths provide a more relaxed experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31365646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polop de la Marina – View of Puig Campana loop from l'Alfàs del Pi&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 34.3 km route with manageable elevation.

Q: Where can I find more information about cycling in Benidorm?
A: For additional details on cycling tourism and infrastructure in the area, you can visit the official Benidorm tourism website: &lt;a href="https://en.visitbenidorm.es/ver/1182/cycling-tourism-in-benidorm.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitbenidorm.es&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
Already an overwhelming feeling to cross a pass so close to the sea that easily cracks the 1,000 meter mark! In the meantime the mountain even seems to have grown by a meter (see pictures). 😉

Regardless of which direction you are going, you can definitely look forward to an impressive descent that will drive the sweat from your face with a fresh airstream. But stay in place for a moment and enjoy the excellent view.

Puerto de Confrides is a very popular route among cyclists on the Costa Blanca, known for its stunning scenery and challenging slopes.
This climb is a category 2 climb, with a length of 9 km and an average gradient of 5%. The initial altitude is 550 m and the final 960 m, with a total gradient of 410 m. The last 1.5 km are the most challenging, with an average gradient of over 7%.
